title: Cremastogyne lanata
taxonomy: Cremastogyne lanata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It belongs to the genus Cremastogyne, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "bromeliad ferns". This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and inflorescence. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, where it grows in shaded areas. As a member of the Asphodelaceae family, Cremastogyne lanata is closely related to other plants like the asphodels and the yuccas.
